channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, KRON4

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Zelenskyy offers to resign in exchange for peace or Ukraine’s NATO membership
item.description: “If it’s about peace in Ukraine, and you really want me to leave my position – I am ready to do that," Zelenskyy told reporters at a press conference in Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Massive brawl erupts at Atlanta airport terminal, viral video shows
item.description: Several travelers were caught on camera tackling one another in a massive brawl that broke out at one of the world's busiest airports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: States threaten fines and jail time for local officials who resist Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: Republican lawmakers in some states are threatening local officials with lawsuits, fines and jail time if they resist President Donald Trump's crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon And David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hawaii man freed after 30 years in prison visits mother's grave and ponders ubiquitous cellphones
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the first places Gordon Cordeiro visited when a judge ordered him released after spending 30 years in prison for a killing he always maintained he had nothing to do with was his mother’s Hawaii gravesite.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One dead, one injured after shooting at New Mexico Air Force base
item.description: Kirtland Air Force base said the 377th Security Forces Squadron was called to an incident at approximately 2 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USDA scholarship for students at historically Black colleges suspended
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal scholarship aimed at boosting students from underserved and rural areas attending historically Black colleges and universities has been put on hold. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Memorial grows for Pennsylvania officer killed in hospital shooting
item.description: A memorial is growing outside the police department in West York, a borough outside of York, Pennsylvania, after officer Andrew Duarte was killed in the line of duty on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY governor makes case for Manhattan tolls in meeting with Trump after feds ordered a halt
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul made the case for Manhattan's congestion tolling during an Oval Office meeting with President Donald Trump after federal officials ordered a halt to the program, a spokesperson for the Democratic governor said.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Quiet Florida neighborhood rocked after three found dead, child kidnapped
item.description: Authorities are investigating a triple homicide involving a Florida man who was also the subject of an Amber Alert for his young daughter last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Killer nightclub hostess's deadly love affair with race car driver was 'eye for an eye': author
item.description: "A Cruel Love: The Ruth Ellis Story," a four-part drama series, was inspired by Carol Ann Lee's true-crime book, "A Fine Day for a Hanging: The Real Ruth Ellis Story.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol
item.description: Enrique Tarrio, the former national chairman of the Proud Boys, was arrested by U.S. Capitol Police on Friday following an altercation with a counter protester.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is buried with military honors
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent who was killed during a traffic stop in Vermont has been buried with full military honors at a national cemetery in Minneapolis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Parents and kids navigate talks of loss and tragedy as they return home after LA area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ceiba Phillips, 11, couldn't believe what he saw when he returned to his Southern California neighborhood a month after a wildfire reduced it to rubble. The ruins of his best friend’s house and his beloved school.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You're going to see more 'city-killer' asteroids, but fret not: MIT astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, ABC 4

item.title: Woman accused of drugging and robbing older men in a deadly romance scheme
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A woman used online dating apps to lure at least four older men to meet her in person, then drugged them with sedatives and stole hundreds of thousands of dollars in a “sinister” romance scheme, FBI officials in Las Vegas said Friday.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL, JESSE BEDAYN and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, ABC 4

item.title: LA mayor dismisses fire chief over response to most destructive wildfire in city history last month
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Six weeks after the start of the most destructive wildfire in city history, Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass ousted the fire chief Friday amid a public rift over preparations for a potential blaze and finger-pointing between the chief and City Hall over responsibility for the devastat
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump signals he’s open to multiple budget bills instead of just one
item.description: Hours after the Senate approved a spending framework, President Donald Trump said in a Fox News interview that he’s open to approving the spending plan in multiple bills “as long as we get to the same point.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan illegal immigrants detained at Guantánamo Bay are flown back home
item.description: Flights that left from Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, Thursday transported nearly 200 illegal immigrants detained on the island back to Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration looks to slash HUD workers tackling the housing crisis
item.description: The Trump administration's proposal to cut half of federal workers at the nation's housing agency is targeting employees who support disaster recovery, rental subsidies, discrimination investigations and first-time homebuyers, according to two documents obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: SALLY HO and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4
